mechanism_narrative: A persistently low FEDFUNDS rate anchors short-end yields and suppresses term-structure expectations, so the 10-year Treasury yield stays lower over the next 20 trading days while the equity market absorbs the easing impulse.
failure_condition: If 10-year yields rise on balance over the next 20 trading days despite FEDFUNDS remaining near the lower bound, the hypothesis is rejected; also reject if triggered easing episodes do not produce lower 20-day Treasury yield changes than non-easing periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration and recession risk, which raises safe-haven demand for Treasuries and pushes the 10-year yield down over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 10-year yields do not decline after UNRATE crosses the 0.3pp/3-month threshold, or if the average 20-day yield change is positive in triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rebound in RSAFS and PAYEMS signals demand stabilization and improving household income, which lifts inflation expectations and nominal Treasury yields over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both increase month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if joint month-over-month gains in RSAFS and PAYEMS are not followed by higher 10-year yields within 20 trading days, or if either series alone explains the sign without the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp fall in INDPRO relative to GDP signals a supply-side shutdown and collapsing real activity, which dominates any inflation impulse and drives the 10-year yield lower over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls while GDPC1 is flat-to-down on the latest available print
failure_condition: Reject if periods with falling INDPRO and flat/down GDPC1 do not show lower 10-year yields over the next 20 trading days, or if yield changes are indistinguishable from zero in those windows.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST posts a trough and then turns up, it marks an eventual housing-demand recovery that raises cyclically sensitive inflation expectations and pushes the 10-year yield higher over a quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if housing upturn signals are not followed by higher 10-year yields within 60 trading days, or if the sign flips negative across repeated housing-recovery episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of CPIAUCSL inflation persistence and a FEDFUNDS rate near zero implies negative real policy rates and expectations of future reflation, which lifts nominal Treasury yields over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL is rising year-over-year while FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-while-near-zero-rate episodes do not precede higher 10-year yields, or if the relationship disappears once FEDFUNDS moves above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: In a payroll-collapse regime, negative PAYEMS momentum combined with rising UNRATE indicates broad labor-market stress and recession pricing, which lowers the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 5 trading days through immediate flight-to-quality demand.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls and UNRATE rises in the same monthly release
failure_condition: Reject if same-month payroll losses with higher unemployment are not followed by lower 10-year yields within 5 trading days, or if the effect only appears without the joint labor deterioration.

mechanism_narrative: A broad demand collapse is present when GDPC1 contracts, RSAFS weakens, and HOUST remains depressed; that trio implies ongoing disinflation and risk aversion, which keeps the 10-year yield lower over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 contracts while RSAFS and HOUST stay below their recent trend
failure_condition: Reject if multi-series demand contraction does not correspond to lower 10-year yields over a 60-day window, or if one series alone accounts for the yield move without the joint pattern.
